articles.title: Delhi Weather: Cool Morning Offers Heatwave Relief; IMD Issues Dust Storm Alert With Light Rainfall - The Daily Jagran
articles.full_text:
- Cool, windy morning provides much-needed heatwave relief to Delhi.
- Dust storms with light drizzles expected in parts of Delhi today
- IMD forecasts light to medium rainfall in Delhi-NCR July 13-19
Delhiites woke up to a cold and windy morning on Tuesday, providing much-needed relief from the heatwave to the residents. Parts of Delhi may experience severe dust storms accompanied by light drizzles. The maximum temperature is expected to go up to 37 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature may fall to 29 degrees Celsius. The national capital is likely to experience light to medium rainfall today. The Indian Meteorological Department forecasts rainfall in some areas of Delhi-NCR between July 13 and 19.
During the last 24 hours, the minimum temperature in the city hovered between 24 and 29 degrees Celsius, while the maximum temperature remained between 37 and 39 degrees Celsius. The maximum humidity was recorded at 76 percent, and the minimum humidity remained at 45 per cent. Winds will blow at a speed of 12 km/h in the city on Tuesday.
Although the IMD has not yet issued a red or orange weather alert, it has advised caution against thunderstorms and strong winds. Dust storms may disrupt traffic. Therefore, wear light clothing and drink plenty of water. Avoid sun exposure between 12 and 4 pm.
Central Delhi Touches 38°C Jahangirpuri Records 28°C
The temperature in Central Delhi is expected to range from 37 degrees Celsius to 38 degrees Celsius. While in Anand Vihar, the temperature was recorded at 31 degrees Celsius. Aerocity stood at 32 degrees Celsius. Alaknanda recorded a temperature of 32 degrees Celsius. Mercury in Abul Fazal Enclave was recorded as 32 degrees Celsius. Ajay Enclave had a temperature of 32 degrees Celsius. Anand Lok: ~32 degrees Celsius. Jahangirpuri and Vasundhara recorded 28 degrees Celsius and 27 degrees Celsius, respectively.
What is Delhi's AQI today?
Delhi's real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) currently ranges between 110 and 170, placing the air quality in the "unhealthy for sensitive groups" to "unhealthy" category, depending on your specific neighborhood.
